---
title: "popdyn model"
format:
  html:
    theme: default
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
---

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Create a network
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = 5, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)

# Initial populations at each node
initial_populations <- c(50, 30, 20, 10, 5)  # Example initial sizes

# Dispersal matrix (rows and columns correspond to nodes)
dispersal_matrix <- matrix(runif(25, 0.01, 0.05), nrow = 5, ncol = 5)
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0.2  # No dispersal to the same node

# Logistic growth model with dispersal
logistic_growth_with_dispersal <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  with(as.list(c(y, parameters)), {
    dN <- rep(0, length(y))
    for(i in 1:length(y)) {
      net_dispersal <- sum(dispersal_matrix[i,] * y) - sum(dispersal_matrix[,i] * y[i])
      dN[i] <- r * y[i] * (1 - y[i]/K) + net_dispersal
    }
    list(dN)
  })
}

# Parameters for the model
parameters <- c(r = 0.1, K = 100)  # Assuming the same for all nodes for simplicity

# Time vector for the simulation
times <- seq(0, 100, by = 1)

# Solve the ODE system for each node, including dispersal
out <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = logistic_growth_with_dispersal, parms = parameters)

# Plot the results
matplot(out[,-1], type = "l", lty = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Population Size",
        main = "Population Dynamics with Dispersal")
legend("topright", legend = paste("Node", 1:5), col = 1:5, lty = 1)

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Number of nodes
n_nodes <- 5

# Create a graph with no edges initially
g <- make_empty_graph(n = n_nodes)

# Random coordinates for nodes in a 100x100 area
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)

# Calculate distances between all pairs of nodes
dist_matrix <- as.matrix(dist(node_coords))
# Adjust dispersal rates inversely proportional to distance, with a scaling factor
dispersal_matrix <- 1 / dist_matrix
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0  # Zero out the diagonal to prevent self-dispersal

# Define the logistic growth model with dispersal
logistic_growth_with_dispersal <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  dN <- rep(0, length(y))
  for (i in 1:length(y)) {
    net_dispersal <- sum(dispersal_matrix[i, ] * y) - y[i] * sum(dispersal_matrix[, i])
    dN[i] <- parameters$r * y[i] * (1 - y[i] / parameters$K) + net_dispersal
  }
  list(dN)
}

# Initial populations, parameters, and time vector
initial_populations <- c(50, 30, 20, 10, 5)
parameters <- list(r = 0.1, K = 100)

# Simulate dynamics
times <- seq(0, 100, by = 1)
out <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = logistic_growth_with_dispersal, parms = parameters)

# Set up plotting area for two plots side by side
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Plotting the spatial network
plot(g, layout = node_coords, main = "Spatial Network", xlab = "X coordinate", ylab = "Y coordinate", vertex.size = initial_populations, vertex.label.cex = 0.7)

# Plotting population dynamics
matplot(out[,-1], type = "l", lty = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Population Size", main = "Population Dynamics", col = 1:n_nodes)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Node", 1:n_nodes), col = 1:n_nodes, lty = 1)

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Define the number of nodes
n_nodes <- 5

# Create a graph with edges to simulate a network structure
# For demonstration, we create a random graph; adjust as needed for your specific network
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = n_nodes, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)

# Random coordinates for nodes in a 100x100 area for plotting purposes
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)
V(g)$x <- node_coords[, 1]
V(g)$y <- node_coords[, 2]

# Use the graph structure to calculate network distances for dispersal
# Here we calculate the shortest path lengths between all pairs of vertices
network_dist_matrix <- distances(g)
# Inverse of network distances for dispersal rates, avoiding division by zero for disconnected components
network_dist_matrix[network_dist_matrix == Inf] <- 0
dispersal_matrix <- 1 / network_dist_matrix
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0  # Zero out the diagonal to prevent self-dispersal

# Define the logistic growth model with network-based dispersal
logistic_growth_with_dispersal <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  dN <- rep(0, length(y))
  for (i in 1:length(y)) {
    net_dispersal <- sum(dispersal_matrix[i, ] * y) - y[i] * sum(dispersal_matrix[, i])
    dN[i] <- parameters$r * y[i] * (1 - y[i] / parameters$K) + net_dispersal
  }
  list(dN)
}

# Initial populations, parameters, and time vector
initial_populations <- c(50, 30, 20, 10, 5)
parameters <- list(r = 0.1, K = 100)

# Simulate dynamics
times <- seq(0, 100, by = 1)
# Assuming previous setup for ode call
out <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = logistic_growth_with_dispersal, parms = parameters, 
           rtol = 1e-6, atol = 1e-6)  # Adjusted tolerances


# Set up plotting area for two plots side by side
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Plotting the spatial network with edges
plot(g, layout = node_coords, main = "Network with Edges", xlab = "X coordinate", ylab = "Y coordinate", 
     vertex.size = 5, vertex.label.cex = 0.7, edge.arrow.size = 0.5)

# Plotting population dynamics
matplot(out[,-1], type = "l", lty = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Population Size", main = "Population Dynamics", col = 1:n_nodes)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Node", 1:n_nodes), col = 1:n_nodes, lty = 1)

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Number of nodes
n_nodes <- 5

# Create a graph with edges to simulate a network structure
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = n_nodes, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)

# Assign random coordinates for plotting
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)
V(g)$x <- node_coords[, 1]
V(g)$y <- node_coords[, 2]

# Assign node-specific r and K values
node_r <- runif(n_nodes, 0.05, 0.15)  # Random growth rates between 0.05 and 0.15
node_K <- runif(n_nodes, 50, 150)     # Random carrying capacities between 50 and 150

# Calculate network distances and adjust dispersal matrix
network_dist_matrix <- distances(g)
network_dist_matrix[network_dist_matrix == Inf] <- 0  # Avoid division by zero
# Introduce variability in dispersal distances
dispersal_matrix <- exp(-network_dist_matrix / mean(network_dist_matrix))  # Example: exponential decay based on average network distance
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0.8  # Prevent self-dispersal

# Define the logistic growth model with variable r, K, and dispersal
logistic_growth_with_variable_params <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  dN <- numeric(length(y))
  for (i in 1:length(y)) {
    net_dispersal <- sum(dispersal_matrix[i, ] * y) - y[i] * sum(dispersal_matrix[, i])
    dN[i] <- parameters$r[i] * y[i] * (1 - y[i] / parameters$K[i]) + net_dispersal
  }
  list(dN)
}

# Initial populations and time vector
initial_populations <- runif(n_nodes, 10, 50)  # Random initial populations
parameters <- list(r = node_r, K = node_K)

# Simulate dynamics with variable parameters and dispersal
times <- seq(0, 100, by = 1)
out <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = logistic_growth_with_variable_params, parms = parameters,
           rtol = 1e-6, atol = 1e-6)  # Using adjusted tolerances

# Set up plotting area
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Plotting the spatial network with edges
plot(g, layout = node_coords, main = "Network with Variable Habitat Quality", xlab = "X coordinate", ylab = "Y coordinate", 
     vertex.size = sqrt(node_K), vertex.label.cex = 0.7, edge.arrow.size = 0.5)

# Plotting population dynamics
matplot(out[,-1], type = "l", lty = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Population Size", main = "Population Dynamics with Variable r and K", col = 1:n_nodes)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Node", 1:n_nodes), col = 1:n_nodes, lty = 1)

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Number of nodes
n_nodes <- 150

# Create a graph with edges to simulate a network structure
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = n_nodes, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)

# Assign random coordinates for plotting
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)
V(g)$x <- node_coords[, 1]
V(g)$y <- node_coords[, 2]

# Assign node-specific r and K values
node_r <- runif(n_nodes, 0.05, 0.15)  # Random growth rates
node_K <- runif(n_nodes, 50, 150)     # Random carrying capacities

# Calculate network distances
network_dist_matrix <- distances(g)
network_dist_matrix[network_dist_matrix == Inf] <- 0  # Adjust for disconnected components

# Shorten the dispersal distances by adjusting the dispersal matrix
decay_coefficient <- 2.2 * mean(network_dist_matrix)  # Increase decay rate
dispersal_matrix <- exp(-network_dist_matrix / decay_coefficient)
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0.8

# Define the logistic growth model with variable r, K, and adjusted dispersal
logistic_growth_with_variable_params <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  dN <- numeric(length(y))
  for (i in 1:length(y)) {
    net_dispersal <- sum(dispersal_matrix[i, ] * y) - y[i] * sum(dispersal_matrix[, i])
    dN[i] <- parameters$r[i] * y[i] * (1 - y[i] / parameters$K[i]) + net_dispersal
  }
  list(dN)
}

# Initial populations and time vector
initial_populations <- runif(n_nodes, 10, 50)  # Random initial populations
parameters <- list(r = node_r, K = node_K)

# Simulate dynamics with adjusted dispersal distances
times <- seq(0, 500, by = 1)
out <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = logistic_growth_with_variable_params, parms = parameters,
           rtol = 1e-6, atol = 1e-6)  # Using adjusted tolerances

# Set up plotting area
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

# Plotting the spatial network with edges
plot(g, layout = node_coords, main = "Network with Variable Habitat Quality", xlab = "X coordinate", ylab = "Y coordinate", 
     vertex.size = sqrt(node_K), vertex.label.cex = 0.7, edge.arrow.size = 0.5)

# Plotting population dynamics with shortened dispersal distances
matplot(out[,-1], type = "l", lty = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Population Size", main = "Population Dynamics with Shortened Dispersal", col = 1:n_nodes)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Node", 1:n_nodes), col = 1:n_nodes, lty = 1)

```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Number of nodes
n_nodes <- 15

# Create a graph with edges to simulate a network structure
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = n_nodes, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)

# Assign random coordinates for plotting
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)
V(g)$x <- node_coords[, 1]
V(g)$y <- node_coords[, 2]

# Assign node-specific r and K values
node_r <- runif(n_nodes, 0.05, 0.15)  # Random growth rates
node_K <- runif(n_nodes, 50, 150)     # Random carrying capacities

# Calculate network distances
network_dist_matrix <- distances(g)
network_dist_matrix[network_dist_matrix == Inf] <- 0  # Adjust for disconnected components

# Adjust dispersal matrix for shortened dispersal distances
decay_coefficient <- 5 * mean(network_dist_matrix)  # Increase decay rate
dispersal_matrix <- exp(-network_dist_matrix / decay_coefficient)
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0

# Define the logistic growth model with variable r, K, and adjusted dispersal
logistic_growth_with_variable_params <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  dN <- numeric(length(y))
  for (i in 1:length(y)) {
    net_dispersal <- sum(dispersal_matrix[i, ] * y) - y[i] * sum(dispersal_matrix[, i])
    dN[i] <- parameters$r[i] * y[i] * (1 - y[i] / parameters$K[i]) + net_dispersal
  }
  list(dN)
}

# Initial populations and time vector
initial_populations <- runif(n_nodes, 10, 50)  # Random initial populations
parameters <- list(r = node_r, K = node_K)

# Simulate dynamics with adjusted dispersal distances
times <- seq(0, 100, by = 1)
out <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = logistic_growth_with_variable_params, parms = parameters,
           rtol = 1e-6, atol = 1e-6)  # Using adjusted tolerances

# Track extinctions and plot results
# Initialize matrix for extinction times (NA indicates no extinction)
extinction_times <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_nodes, ncol = 2)
colnames(extinction_times) <- c("Node", "Time")

for (i in 1:n_nodes) {
  extinct_idx <- which(out[, i + 1] <= 1e-3)[1]  # Adding 1 because the first column of `out` is time
  if (length(extinct_idx) > 0) {
    extinction_times[i, ] <- c(i, out[extinct_idx, 1])
  }
}

# Filter out nodes that did not go extinct
extinct_nodes <- extinction_times[!is.na(extinction_times[, "Time"]), ]

# Adjust plotting area for three figures
par(mfrow = c(1, 3))

# Plot 1: Spatial network with edges
plot(g, layout = node_coords, main = "Network with Variable Habitat", xlab = "X", ylab = "Y", 
     vertex.size = sqrt(node_K), vertex.label.cex = 0.7, edge.arrow.size = 0.5)

# Plot 2: Population dynamics
matplot(out[,-1], type = "l", lty = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Population", main = "Population Dynamics", col = 1:n_nodes)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Node", 1:n_nodes), col = 1:n_nodes, lty = 1)

# Plot 3: Extinction times
if (nrow(extinct_nodes) > 0) {
  plot(extinct_nodes[, "Node"], extinct_nodes[, "Time"], xlim = c(1, n_nodes), ylim = c(0, max(times)), xlab = "Node", ylab = "Time of Extinction",
       main = "Extinction Times", pch = 19, col = 'red')
  abline(h = extinct_nodes[, "Time"], col = "gray", lty = 3)
} else {
  plot(1, type = "n", xlim = c(1, n_nodes), ylim = c(0, max(times)), xlab = "Node", ylab = "Time of Extinction",
       main = "Extinction Times")
  text(x = mean(1:n_nodes), y = max(times) / 2, labels = "No Extinctions")
}

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Define the number of nodes and create a graph with edges
n_nodes <- 15
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = n_nodes, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)

# Assign random coordinates for plotting and node-specific r and K values
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)
node_r <- runif(n_nodes, 0.05, 0.15)  # Random growth rates
node_K <- runif(n_nodes, 50, 150)     # Random carrying capacities

V(g)$x <- node_coords[, 1]
V(g)$y <- node_coords[, 2]

# Calculate network distances and adjust dispersal matrix for shortened distances
network_dist_matrix <- distances(g)
network_dist_matrix[network_dist_matrix == Inf] <- 0
decay_coefficient <- 12 * mean(network_dist_matrix)
dispersal_matrix <- exp(-network_dist_matrix / decay_coefficient)
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0

# Add demographic and dispersal stochasticity to the logistic growth function
logistic_growth_with_stochasticity <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  dN <- numeric(length(y))
  for (i in 1:length(y)) {
    r_stochastic <- rnorm(1, parameters$r[i], sd = 0.01)  # Demographic stochasticity
    dispersal_stochastic <- dispersal_matrix[i, ] * rnorm(n_nodes, 1, sd = 0.05)  # Dispersal stochasticity
    net_dispersal <- sum(dispersal_stochastic * y) - y[i] * sum(dispersal_stochastic)
    dN[i] <- r_stochastic * y[i] * (1 - y[i] / parameters$K[i]) + net_dispersal
  }
  list(dN)
}

# Initial populations and simulation parameters
initial_populations <- runif(n_nodes, 10, 50)
parameters <- list(r = node_r, K = node_K)

# Simulate dynamics with stochastic elements
set.seed(123)  # Ensure reproducibility
times <- seq(0, 100, by = 1)
out_stochastic <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = logistic_growth_with_stochasticity, parms = parameters,
                      rtol = 1e-6, atol = 1e-6)

# Plotting setup
par(mfrow = c(1, 3))

# Plot 1: Spatial network with edges
plot(g, layout = node_coords, main = "Network with Variable Habitat", xlab = "X", ylab = "Y",
     vertex.size = sqrt(node_K), vertex.label.cex = 0.7, edge.arrow.size = 0.5)

# Plot 2: Stochastic Population Dynamics
matplot(out_stochastic[,-1], type = "l", lty = 1, xlab = "Time", ylab = "Population", main = "Stochastic Dynamics", col = 1:n_nodes)
legend("topright", legend = paste("Node", 1:n_nodes), col = 1:n_nodes, lty = 1)

# Plot 3: Extinction times recalculated for the stochastic model
extinction_times_stochastic <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_nodes, ncol = 2)
colnames(extinction_times_stochastic) <- c("Node", "Time")
for (i in 1:n_nodes) {
  extinct_idx <- which(out_stochastic[, i + 1] <= 1e-3)[1]
  if (length(extinct_idx) > 0) {
    extinction_times_stochastic[i, ] <- c(i, out_stochastic[extinct_idx, 1])
  }
}
extinct_nodes_stochastic <- extinction_times_stochastic[!is.na(extinction_times_stochastic[, "Time"]), ]

if (nrow(extinct_nodes_stochastic) > 0) {
  plot(extinct_nodes_stochastic[, "Node"], extinct_nodes_stochastic[, "Time"], xlim = c(1, n_nodes), ylim = c(0, max(times)), xlab = "Node", ylab = "Time of Extinction",
       main = "Extinction Times", pch = 19, col = 'red')
  abline(h = extinct_nodes_stochastic[, "Time"], col = "gray", lty = 3)
} else {
  plot(1, type = "n", xlim = c(1, n_nodes), ylim = c(0, max(times)), xlab = "Node", ylab = "Time of Extinction",
       main = "Extinction Times")
  text(x = mean(1:n_nodes), y = max(times) / 2, labels = "No Extinctions")
}

```


```{r, cache=TRUE}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)

# Define the number of nodes and species
n_nodes <- 15
n_species <- 3

# Create a graph with edges to simulate a network structure
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = n_nodes, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)

# Assign random coordinates for plotting and node-specific r and K values for each species
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)
species_r <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * n_species, 0.01, 0.1), nrow = n_nodes, ncol = n_species)
species_K <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * n_species, 50, 200), nrow = n_nodes, ncol = n_species)

# Assign positions for plotting
V(g)$x <- node_coords[, 1]
V(g)$y <- node_coords[, 2]

# Calculate network distances and adjust dispersal matrix for shortened distances
network_dist_matrix <- distances(g)
network_dist_matrix[network_dist_matrix == Inf] <- 0
decay_coefficient <- 2 * mean(network_dist_matrix) # Adjust for shortened dispersal distances
dispersal_matrix <- exp(-network_dist_matrix / decay_coefficient)
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0

# Define species interactions through a competition matrix
competition_matrix <- matrix(runif(n_species * n_species, 0, 0.1), nrow = n_species, ncol = n_species)
diag(competition_matrix) <- 0  # No self-competition

print(is.matrix(competition_matrix))
print(dim(competition_matrix))

# Update the community dynamics model to include species interactions and stochastic elements
community_dynamics <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  # Your existing setup and computations inside the function
  
  y_matrix <- matrix(y, nrow = n_nodes, byrow = TRUE)
  dN_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = n_nodes, ncol = n_species)
  
  for (i in 1:n_nodes) {
    for (j in 1:n_species) {
      r_stochastic <- rnorm(1, species_r[i, j], sd = 0.01) # Demographic stochasticity
      competition_effect <- sum(competition_matrix[j, ] * y_matrix[i, ]) # Competition
      dN_matrix[i, j] <- r_stochastic * y_matrix[i, j] * (1 - (y_matrix[i, j] + competition_effect) / species_K[i, j]) # Net change
    }
  }
  
  # Return the derivatives as a list
  return(list(as.vector(dN_matrix)))
}

# Initial populations for each species at each node
initial_populations <- runif(n_nodes * n_species, 10, 50)

# Simulate dynamics with the community model
set.seed(123)  # For reproducibility
times <- seq(0, 300, by = 1)

out_community <- ode(
  y = initial_populations,
  times = times,
  func = community_dynamics,
  parms = list(),
  rtol = 1e-6,
  atol = rep(1e-6, n_nodes * n_species),
  maxsteps = 1e8
)




#out_community <- ode(y = initial_populations, times = times, func = community_dynamics, parms = list(),
#                     rtol = 1e-6, atol = rep(1e-6, n_nodes * n_species))

# Summarize and plot total population over time as a demonstration
total_population <- rowSums(matrix(out_community[,-1], ncol = n_species, byrow = TRUE))

# Plotting the total population over time
plot(out_community[, 1], total_population, type = 'l', xlab = "Time", ylab = "Total Population", main = "Community Dynamics Summary")


```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(igraph)
library(deSolve)
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)

# Set up parallel backend
numCores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1
registerDoParallel(cores = numCores)

# Define the number of nodes and species
n_nodes <- 5
n_species <- 3

# Create a graph, assign coordinates, node-specific parameters, and compute matrices
g <- erdos.renyi.game(n = n_nodes, p = 0.5, type = "gnp", directed = FALSE)
node_coords <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * 2, 0, 100), ncol = 2)
species_r <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * n_species, 0.01, 0.1), nrow = n_nodes, ncol = n_species)
species_K <- matrix(runif(n_nodes * n_species, 50, 200), nrow = n_nodes, ncol = n_species)
V(g)$x <- node_coords[, 1]
V(g)$y <- node_coords[, 2]
network_dist_matrix <- distances(g)
network_dist_matrix[network_dist_matrix == Inf] <- 0
decay_coefficient <- 2 * mean(network_dist_matrix)
dispersal_matrix <- exp(-network_dist_matrix / decay_coefficient)
diag(dispersal_matrix) <- 0
competition_matrix <- matrix(runif(n_species * n_species, 0, 0.1), nrow = n_species, ncol = n_species)
diag(competition_matrix) <- 0

# Model function with parallel computation
community_dynamics <- function(t, y, parameters) {
  y_matrix <- matrix(y, nrow = n_nodes, byrow = TRUE)
  
  results <- foreach(i = 1:n_nodes, .combine = 'rbind') %dopar% {
    rowResults <- numeric(n_species)
    for (j in 1:n_species) {
      r_stochastic <- rnorm(1, species_r[i, j], sd = 0.01)
      competition_effect <- sum(competition_matrix[j, ] * y_matrix[i, ])
      rowResults[j] <- r_stochastic * y_matrix[i, j] * (1 - (y_matrix[i, j] + competition_effect) / species_K[i, j])
    }
    return(rowResults)
  }
  
  dN_matrix <- matrix(unlist(results), nrow = n_nodes, byrow = TRUE)
  return(list(as.vector(dN_matrix)))
}

# Initial populations and run simulation
initial_populations <- runif(n_nodes * n_species, 10, 50)
set.seed(123)
times <- seq(0, 300, by = 1)

out_community <- ode(
  y = initial_populations,
  times = times,
  func = community_dynamics,
  parms = list(),
  rtol = 1e-6,
  atol = rep(1e-6, n_nodes * n_species),
  maxsteps = 1e8
)

# Summarize and visualize results
total_population <- rowSums(matrix(out_community[,-1], ncol = n_species, byrow = TRUE))
plot(out_community[, 1], total_population, type = 'l', xlab = "Time", ylab = "Total Population", main = "Community Dynamics Summary")

```
